prologueScenes.text: This is Watson Manor School. It looks like any other school — classrooms, a gym, a library, a canteen.
But this building is old. Very old. It was built in 1898 as a manor — a big, grand house where a rich family once lived. They called it Watson Manor.
In 1952, the manor became a school. But parts of the old house are still here — the stone cellar, the wooden library, the courtyard with the fountain.

The highlight of every school year is Sports Week in June — a whole week of competitions. Running, swimming, cycling, climbing. The winner gets the Watson Manor Trophy — and their name is engraved on it forever.
In the entrance hall, there is a glass case. Inside it: the Watson Manor Trophy. A small bronze cup with the names of every Sports Week winner since 1960. Sixty-three years of champions.
This year, on the first day of school, the headmaster opens the case. But the trophy is gone. The glass is not broken. The lock is not damaged. Someone took it — and left no trace.
Well… almost no trace. There is a tiny piece of green fabric caught on the hinge. And two 2nd-graders have already noticed it.
Their names are Nora and Elias. And this is their story.

campaignEvidence.title: Green Fabric (Sample A)
campaignEvidence.desc: Tiny piece of dark green cotton on the hinge. The first clue. Could be from a jacket or cardigan.

campaignEvidence.title: Mr. Kramer Interview (4 PM, Sept 2)
campaignEvidence.desc: He named Coach Martinez. Looked at the floor. Embarrassed about "differences in June".

campaignEvidence.title: The Empty Hooks
campaignEvidence.desc: Staff trophy cabinet. Two hooks empty where Coach Martinez's engraving tools used to hang.

campaignEvidence.title: Sports Week 2025 — Two Pages
campaignEvidence.desc: Qualifying heats: Lukas Brunner 27.4s. Final result: Tobias Kramer 30.9s. Two handwritings. One lie.

campaignEvidence.title: 2010 HR Footnote
campaignEvidence.desc: Master key issued to T. Martinez, 2010-04-12. Returned: blank. He has had a key for sixteen years.

campaignEvidence.title: Coach Martinez at the Junior Club
campaignEvidence.desc: Joined the club in July. Knows the trophy is missing. Asked us how Lukas Brunner is. Stopped smiling.

campaignEvidence.title: Felix's Letter (May 12) + the Lukas Connection
campaignEvidence.desc: Felix wrote a letter. Coach Martinez was the only adult who replied. Met three times. Felix's brother Lukas is the boy from the 2025 chronicle.

campaignEvidence.title: Cellar Log Entry — 30 June 17:42
campaignEvidence.desc: Coach Martinez signed out a long box on his last working day. The trophy fits inside a long box.

campaignEvidence.title: Mrs. Brennan's Leaving-Do Photo
campaignEvidence.desc: Coach Martinez stood by the trophy case for ten minutes. Lukas Brunner stood silent next to him. Mr. Kramer didn't come.

campaignEvidence.title: Mrs. Schmidt's Open-Evening Video
campaignEvidence.desc: Last clear sighting of the trophy: 20 June 2025 at 19:43. After that, no confirmation all summer.

campaignEvidence.title: The Evidence Wall
campaignEvidence.desc: Two trails above (Coach Martinez took it; Felix covered for him). One root underneath (Mr. Kramer fixed the 2025 200m and stole Lukas Brunner's win).

campaignEvidence.title: Felix Brunner's Confession (Sept 8, 17:30)
campaignEvidence.desc: Felix is Lukas's brother. Coach Martinez told him about the 2025 fix in May. Felix planted everything to give the coach time to engrave Lukas's name on the trophy.

campaignEvidence.title: Mr. Kramer's Phone Call (Sept 9, 4:30 PM)
campaignEvidence.desc: Mr. Kramer could not meet Felix's eyes when he heard Lukas's name. Then he called Coach Martinez.

campaignEvidence.title: The Trophy on Linden Street
campaignEvidence.desc: Polished. Engraved "LUKAS BRUNNER — 2025 — 27.4s" in Coach Martinez's handwriting. Mr. Kramer read it and went pale. School assembly tomorrow.

campaignEvidence.title: The Watson Weekly — Sports Week Special
campaignEvidence.desc: 2025 result corrected. Mr. Kramer apologises. Lukas Brunner's name engraved on the trophy. Three young detectives at the assembly.

Watson Manor School Newsletter — SPECIAL REPORT

The Watson Manor Trophy is missing. The headmaster, Mr. Kramer, discovered the empty case on Monday morning at the start-of-year announcement in the school hall. The trophy was last seen in June, at the end of last school year.

The glass case was not broken. The lock was not damaged. Two police officers visited but said the trophy has low financial value. They are not actively investigating. The school community is upset.

Suspect File: Felix Novak

Age: 13. Class: 3B. Father: Mr. Novak, owner of Novak and Son Antiques.

The antique shop is on Park Street, opposite the park, two minutes from the school gate. It was open all summer.

Felix helped his father during the holidays. He picked up and delivered items. He had access to a van and probably had keys to the shop.

At the adventure park, Felix did not join any activities. When I asked about his summer, he changed the subject. He avoids talking about the school building.

Status: Under investigation. No proof yet.

tasks.passage:
Field Notes — Novak and Son Antiques, Saturday 11:15 AM

The shop was closed. A handwritten sign said 'Open Mon-Fri, 9-5.' We looked through the front window.

The main room has shelves with old clocks, ceramic vases, framed paintings, and small bronze statues. Everything looks expensive and well-organised.

In the back room (partly visible through an open door), I could see more shelves. On the second shelf from the top, there was a small bronze object. It could be a cup or a trophy — but I could not see clearly because of the distance and the glass reflection.

We need to get inside. Legally.

Interview Notes — Mr. Novak, Antique Dealer

Mr. Novak has owned his shop on Park Street for fifteen years. He buys, sells, and restores old furniture, paintings, and other valuable objects.

He told me that schools and museums sometimes ask him to clean or repair old items. 'Watson Manor School asks us to polish the trophy every summer,' he said. 'Beautiful piece.'

He said his son Felix helps with pickups during the holidays. 'He is a good boy. He takes the van sometimes and collects items for me.'

When I asked about the trophy, Mr. Novak looked confused. 'A trophy? The trophy? Yes, it should be at the shop. I got the museum commission and completely forgot to return it.'

tasks.passage:
THE EVIDENCE WALL

REAL TRAIL — what Coach Martinez did:

  1. 2010 master key issued to him. Never returned.
  2. Engraving tools taken from the staff cabinet at the end of June.
  3. 30 June, 17:42: signs out a long box from the cellar.
  4. Mrs. Brennan's photo: stood by the trophy at the leaving-do. Lukas Brunner beside him.

PLANTED TRAIL — what Felix did afterwards:

  1. Green fabric on the hinge (28 August open evening).
  2. Rumour: Leo Winkler at school at night (false).
  3. Cardigan threads in Felix's own locker match the fabric.

ROOT CAUSE — what Mr. Kramer did in June 2025:

Mr. Kramer fixed the 2025 200m result. Lukas Brunner's qualifying time was 27.4s. Tobias Kramer was recorded as the winner at 30.9s. The chronicle page is in Mr. Kramer's handwriting.

Statement of Felix Brunner

Sept 8, 17:30

I have just told Nora and Elias everything.

Coach Martinez trained my brother Lukas for two years. In June 2025 Lukas ran the 200m. He was the fastest. He should have won. But Mr. Kramer recorded his nephew Tobias as the winner. Lukas has not run since.

In May, Coach Martinez met me three times. He told me about Lukas. He showed me the qualifying-heat times. He said he was going to put my brother's name back on the trophy.

On the first day of school, I saw the empty case. I knew. So I tore my cardigan, planted the fabric, and made up the rumours. I have never told Coach Martinez that I did any of it.

tasks.passage:
Statement by Felix Novak — Mr. Kramer's Office

My dad's shop has polished the Watson Manor Trophy every summer for ten years. I picked it up in July, like always. But my dad got a huge museum order and forgot to polish and return the school trophy.

When school started and Mr. Kramer said the trophy was missing, I panicked. Everyone knows I hate Sports Week. I wrote a letter last year asking to cancel it. I was sure everyone would blame me.

So I planted false evidence. I tore a piece of my green jacket and placed it on the trophy case hinge to make it look like a break-in. I spread rumours about Leo being at school at night. I told Mrs. Brennan about the caretaker's keys to make Rudi look suspicious.

I watched Nora and Elias follow my false trails for weeks. I felt terrible every day. I am sorry for all of it — for the lies, for trying to blame Leo and Rudi, and for not telling the truth sooner.

tasks.passage:
The Garage

Coach Martinez's garage smelled of metal polish. The trophy stood on his workbench, gleaming. The engraving tools lay beside it.

He lifted the trophy gently. "I have already engraved Lukas's name," he said. "Read it."

Mr. Kramer read the engraving. 2025. 200m. LUKAS BRUNNER. 27.4 seconds. He went pale.

"The trophy goes back," said Coach Martinez. "Lukas's name stays on it. There will be a school assembly tomorrow morning."

"Yes," said Mr. Kramer, very quietly. "I will tell the school."

"Felix," said Coach Martinez. "I have never asked you to lie for me. But I am glad you are your brother's brother."

Seaview Animal Shelter — Incident Report

Date: Community Day. Three animals were reported missing this morning: Mist (grey cat), Hoppy (brown rabbit), and Herbert (tortoise, very old).

The night volunteer locked all enclosures at 10 PM. The front gate was still locked this morning. No signs of a break-in.

Three student volunteers from Watson Manor School investigated. They discovered a gap in the garden fence, approximately 15 cm wide. Cat and rabbit tracks led through the gap. Tortoise tracks led along the inside of the fence to the gap and then onto the pavement outside.

The students found Mist under a car on Park Street, Hoppy in a garden on Elm Road eating lettuce, and Herbert on the pavement just outside the shelter gate. All three animals were returned safely.

The fence gap has been repaired. The shelter thanks the Investigation Club for their quick work.
